hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 pockets, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will reduce off-target SSTR3/SSTR5 binding by avoiding off-target-conserved hydrophilic interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs achieve strong SSTR2 binding but fail to disrupt off-target interactions. The next iteration should prioritize mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to improve selectivity over binding strength.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target binding (particularly SSTR3 and SSTR5) and are not part of the pharmacophore. Focusing mutations on these positions can disrupt off-target interactions while preserving the native pharmacophore (FWKT(7-10) and Cys3/Cys14).

Introducing mutations at these positions with amino acids that are less likely to form conserved off-target interactions (e.g., A, G, T) can reduce off-target binding. These mutations are expected to disrupt interactions in SSTR3 and SSTR5 without affecting the pharmacophore.
